bugs_fixed.title: Tool calls fail with missing required parameter on Responses API streaming
bugs_fixed.root_cause: In parse_responses_api_stream_chunk (parsing.rs), the response.output_item.done handler for function_call items used item.get("index") to determine the accumulator slot. The Responses API puts the output array position in the top-level event field "output_index", not inside the item object. For reasoning models that produce [reasoning(index=0), function_call(index=1)], output_item.done would emit StreamToolCall{index: 0} (defaulting because item.index is absent), updating the wrong accumulator. Meanwhile, the argument deltas correctly targeted index=1 via output_index. Result: accumulator at index=0 got name/id but no arguments; accumulator at index=1 got arguments but no name. finalize_tool_calls filters accumulators with empty names, so index=1 was dropped and index=0 produced empty arguments={}, triggering 'missing required parameter'.
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed response.output_item.done function_call index lookup to use payload.get("output_index") first (same as output_item.added and delta events), with fallback to payload.get("index") then item.get("index"). This matches the correct position for all Responses API event types.

bugs_fixed.title: Context window shows incorrect percentage for GPT-5.4
bugs_fixed.root_cause: The compiled-in registry already had gpt-5.4 with context_window=1048576. However, the AgentConfig.token_limit was hardcoded to 128_000 in stack_run.rs for both the main agent run and sub-agent runs. This value drives context compaction thresholds (ContextManager). For a model with 1M context, compaction would trigger at 128K*0.75=96K tokens instead of 750K. The 25% display at 50K tokens aligns with a 200K context window — the default used by instruction_resolver when a model is not found. If the model name used at runtime differs slightly from the registry entry, lookup_context_window returns None and the status bar shows no percentage, but the compaction logic using 128K would fire prematurely. The token_limit now correctly reads the model's context_window from the registry.
bugs_fixed.fix: In stack_run.rs, replaced hardcoded token_limit: 128_000 with a registry lookup that returns the model's actual context_window (falling back to 128K for unknown models). Applied to both main agent runs and sub-agent runs.

root_cause: Two independent bugs: (1) Wrong index resolution in Responses API stream accumulator for output_item.done function_call events caused arguments to be accumulated at the wrong slot, producing empty tool call arguments. (2) Hardcoded token_limit=128K ignored the model's actual context window from the registry, causing premature context compaction for large-context models like GPT-5.4 (1M tokens).
